note: Sprint 5.32 Trinity Materials Engine Deterministic Backend v0.1: lands the FIRST non-placeholder, non-toy backend in the Useful Compute worker — local_materials_engine_v01 — and with it the first ever real_backend kind. Reads Sprint 5.31 classifier metadata in the request (metadata.scientific_task_classification.candidate_materials / candidate_metrics / task_kind), looks each material up in a curated local properties table pinned in scripts/trinity/useful_compute_backends.py (CeO2 / PrOx / Sm2O3 / Y2O3 / ZrO2 / TiO2 with six properties each: oxygen_storage_mmol_g, optimal_temperature_c, redox_support, stability, conductivity, surface_area_m2_g), scores each requested metric via a metric→property mapping (_METRIC_TO_PROPERTY) plus normalised property bounds (_PROPERTY_BOUNDS), and emits a ranked materials comparison. NOT DFT. NOT quantum. NOT a real simulation. Property values are explicitly labelled as illustrative in the result's `limitations` field. Auto-router added to scripts/trinity/useful_compute_worker.py: when operator runs the queue / operator_loop with default --backend placeholder AND the request carries source_tool=materials_engine + scientific_task_classification metadata, worker transparently upgrades to local_materials_engine_v01; otherwise placeholder stays. Operator can opt out by explicitly passing --backend placeholder_scientific_intake. New schema schemas/trinity/materials_engine_result.schema.json ($id=trinity-materials-engine-result/v0.1) const-locks schema, backend, backend_version, mode; locks task_kind enum (comparison/extraction/validation/benchmark); locks direction enum (higher_is_better/lower_is_better); requires score and normalised_score in [0,1]; source_request_sha256 64-hex; marker_hex 16-hex; classification_id either ^scl-[0-9a-f]{16}$ or empty. Scoring algorithm: normalised_score = (value - lo) / (hi - lo) clamped to [0,1], inverted when direction=lower_is_better; material score = arithmetic mean of metric normalised_scores; ranking sorts by score descending with material name as tiebreaker. Deterministic: two workers over same classifier-derived request produce same compute_output_sha256, preserving the cross-worker replay contract from Sprint 5.12+. Unknown materials are warned and recorded in unknown_materials; ranking still emits for the known set; no crash. Existing test_no_backend_uses_real_backend_kind_in_v01 renamed to test_only_materials_engine_uses_real_backend_kind_in_v01 with an allowlist of one — future real backends still need a sprint that audits the contract. The replay validator, governance gate, reward budget policy, payment proposal and payment draft all flow through unchanged because they were already hash-based; the backend dispatch arm in run_backend handles REAL_BACKEND_KIND for the first time. Static safety enforces zero new network / shell / subprocess / eval / LLM-client tokens (anthropic / openai / langchain / transformers / llama_cpp banned) in both backends and worker. Curated table is pinned in source for reviewability; v0.2 may move it to data/trinity/materials_engine/ with sha256 lock. Tests: 1289 passed, 38 skipped on the deployment host (1250 prior + 39 new = 14 functional + 13 schema + 11 safety + 1 edited reservation-guard). Manual demo over Sprint 5.31 final classifier output: ranking PrOx > CeO2 on oxygen_storage_capacity + temperature_c (PrOx wins on both metrics due to higher OSC + lower optimal temperature); compute_output_sha256 stable across workers; backend_name=local_materials_engine_v01, backend_kind=real_backend; queue completes with governor_decisions_count=7 and watchdog_safety_status=ok. Non-goals for v0.1 (documented): no DFT, no quantum, no real simulation, no network, no LLM, no subprocess from the backend, no new task_type / source_tool enum values, no external properties database, no measured-data claims. Pure scripts+schemas+docs+tests merge: zero src/, zero consensus changes, zero wallet/payment/broadcast changes.
